questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "The Wild, the Innocent & The E Street Shuffle" in 1973?

questions.prompt: In which year was "Souvenir Album" by Ella Fitzgerald first released?

questions.prompt: Which of these albums is by Eminem?

questions.prompt: Which of these albums is by Depeche Mode?

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Kill ’Em All" in 1983?

questions.prompt: Which of these albums is by Johann Sebastian Bach?

questions.prompt: In which year was "Please Please Me" by The Beatles first released?

questions.prompt: In which year was "Ray of Light" by Madonna first released?
questions.explanation: Ray of Light marked a major stylistic shift for Madonna toward electronic music, produced with William Orbit.

questions.prompt: In which year was "The Charlie Parker Story" by Charlie Parker first released?
questions.explanation: The Charlie Parker Story compiled recordings from Parker, the influential bebop saxophonist who died in 1955.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Good Ass Job" in 2009?
questions.explanation: Good Ass Job was released as a compilation gathering Kanye West's guest verses and collaborations rather than a traditional studio album.

questions.prompt: In which year was "The Sound of Johnny Cash" by Johnny Cash first released?
questions.explanation: Johnny Cash was building his reputation with Sun Records and then Columbia during this stretch of his early career.

questions.prompt: Which of these albums is by Björk?
questions.explanation: Volta featured Björk experimenting with brass arrangements and collaborations with producers Timbaland and Antony Hegarty.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "ARCHE" in 2014?
questions.explanation: ARCHE marked a return to a heavier, more aggressive sound for DIR EN GREY after some more experimental releases.

questions.prompt: In which year was "I Never Loved a Man the Way I Love You" by Aretha Franklin first released?
questions.explanation: I Never Loved a Man the Way I Love You was Aretha Franklin's breakthrough album after she moved to Atlantic Records.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Black Roses" in 1986?
questions.explanation: Inner Circle built their reggae sound through the 1970s and 80s before finding mainstream crossover success in the 1990s.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Lawn Boy" by Phish first released?
questions.explanation: Lawn Boy was Phish's second studio album and included fan favorites like "Bouncing Around the Room."

questions.prompt: In which year was "McCartney" by Paul McCartney first released?
questions.explanation: McCartney was recorded largely at home, with Paul McCartney playing almost every instrument himself.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Ballad for Americans" in 1940?
questions.explanation: Ballad for Americans was a patriotic cantata that became a popular recording subject for several artists around 1940.

questions.prompt: In which year was "3 Feet High and Rising" by De La Soul first released?
questions.explanation: 3 Feet High and Rising introduced De La Soul's playful, sample-heavy style and helped popularize alternative hip-hop.

questions.prompt: Which of these albums is by Willie Nelson?
choices: The Lure of the Grand Canyon
questions.explanation: ...And Then I Wrote was Willie Nelson's debut album, showcasing songs he had written that became hits for other country artists.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Speak & Spell" by Depeche Mode first released?
questions.explanation: Speak & Spell was Depeche Mode's only album to feature founding songwriter Vince Clarke, who left soon after to form Yazoo.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Motörhead" by Motörhead first released?
questions.explanation: Motörhead's self-titled album helped define the raw, high-speed sound that influenced the development of thrash metal.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Little Girl Blue" by Nina Simone first released?
questions.explanation: Little Girl Blue was Nina Simone's debut album and included her breakthrough recording of "I Loves You, Porgy."

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Africa's Blood" in 1971?
questions.explanation: Lee "Scratch" Perry ran the legendary Black Ark studio, where he developed much of his groundbreaking dub production style.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "October" in 1981?
questions.explanation: October was U2's second album, shaped in part by the members' involvement with a Christian fellowship group around that time.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Surfer Girl" by The Beach Boys first released?
questions.explanation: Surfer Girl was the first Beach Boys album where Brian Wilson took on production duties himself.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Paris 1934" in 1947?
questions.explanation: Louis Armstrong helped shape the sound of early jazz as one of its most influential trumpeters and vocalists.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Get Rich or Die Tryin’" by 50 Cent first released?
questions.explanation: Get Rich or Die Tryin' was 50 Cent's major-label debut, released after he survived being shot nine times in 2000.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Singing Down The Lane" in 1956?
questions.explanation: Jim Reeves crossed over from honky-tonk into a smoother, pop-influenced country style during this period of his career.

questions.prompt: In which year was "The Bends" by Radiohead first released?
questions.explanation: The Bends came out in March 1995 and marked Radiohead's shift toward the more atmospheric sound that led into OK Computer.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Sehnsucht" in 1997?
questions.explanation: Sehnsucht was Rammstein's second album and their commercial breakthrough, later boosted internationally by the single "Du Hast."

questions.prompt: In which year was "The Temptations Sing Smokey" by The Temptations first released?
questions.explanation: The Temptations Sing Smokey was a tribute album covering songs written by Motown's Smokey Robinson.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Jimmy Cliff / Wonderful World, Beautiful People" by Jimmy Cliff first released?
questions.explanation: Jimmy Cliff helped bring reggae to international audiences during this stretch of his early career.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Ummagumma" in 1969?
questions.explanation: Ummagumma was a double album pairing live Pink Floyd recordings with solo studio compositions from each band member.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Hunky Dory" by David Bowie first released?
questions.explanation: Hunky Dory included "Life on Mars?" and "Changes," two songs that became enduring David Bowie staples.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Young Man With a Horn" in 1952?
questions.explanation: Miles Davis continued refining his early cool jazz sound in the years following his work with Charlie Parker's quintet.

questions.prompt: Which of these albums is by Beastie Boys?
questions.explanation: Check Your Head marked the Beastie Boys playing their own instruments again, moving away from the sample-heavy sound of Paul's Boutique.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Just Between the Two of Us" in 1966?
questions.explanation: Merle Haggard built his early reputation on hard-edged honky-tonk songs drawn from his own working-class background.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Everything Is Wrong" in 1995?
questions.explanation: Everything Is Wrong was Moby's major-label debut, blending electronic dance music with a wide range of other styles.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Follow the Leader" by Korn first released?
questions.explanation: Follow the Leader became Korn's commercial breakthrough, featuring the hit single "Freak on a Leash."

questions.prompt: Which of these albums is by Stevie Wonder?
questions.explanation: Tribute to Uncle Ray was one of Stevie Wonder's earliest albums, recorded as a child prodigy signed to Motown as "Little Stevie Wonder."

questions.prompt: In which year was "Dub Me" by King Tubby first released?
questions.explanation: King Tubby is widely credited as one of the founding architects of dub through his pioneering remix and mixing-desk techniques.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Darkness on the Edge of Town" by Bruce Springsteen first released?
questions.explanation: Darkness on the Edge of Town followed a lengthy legal dispute that kept Bruce Springsteen out of the studio for several years.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Red" in 2012?
questions.explanation: Red included the song "I Knew You Were Trouble," which pushed Taylor Swift's sound further toward pop and electronic production.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Solitude" by Billie Holiday first released?
questions.explanation: Solitude gathered recordings that showcased the more world-weary vocal style of Billie Holiday's later career.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Trap‐a‐Thon" in 2007?
questions.explanation: Gucci Mane released Trap-a-Thon early in his career as he was building the trap subgenre's foundations in Atlanta hip-hop.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Waylon Sings Ol’ Harlan" by Waylon Jennings first released?
questions.explanation: Waylon Jennings was still developing his sound in Nashville before becoming a leading figure of the outlaw country movement.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Born to Die Demos" in 2012?
questions.explanation: Born to Die Demos gathered early versions of songs from Lana Del Rey's breakthrough album Born to Die.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Withering to death." by DIR EN GREY first released?
questions.explanation: Withering to death. saw DIR EN GREY moving toward a heavier, more extreme sound within Japanese visual kei metal.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Showtime" in 1964?
questions.explanation: James Brown's live performances during this era were central to building his reputation as a dynamic showman.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Face To Face" by Beenie Man first released?
questions.explanation: Beenie Man became one of dancehall's most prominent artists through the 1990s, known for his rapid-fire deejay style.

questions.prompt: In which year was "The Rolling Stones No. 2" by The Rolling Stones first released?
questions.explanation: The Rolling Stones No. 2 was the British title for the band's second studio album, released under a different title in the US.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "A Winter Romance" in 1959?
questions.explanation: Dean Martin recorded several seasonal and romance-themed albums like this one during his career as a popular crooner.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Love Is the Thing" by Nat King Cole first released?
questions.explanation: Love Is the Thing became one of Nat King Cole's best-selling albums, built around lush orchestral arrangements by Gordon Jenkins.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Muse Sick‐N‐Hour Mess Age" in 1994?
questions.explanation: Muse Sick-N-Hour Mess Age found Public Enemy addressing criticism of hip-hop and the music industry directly in its lyrics.

questions.prompt: In which year was "The Star Carol" by Tennessee Ernie Ford first released?
questions.explanation: The Star Carol was one of several Christmas and gospel-themed albums Tennessee Ernie Ford recorded during his career.

questions.prompt: Which of these albums is by Kraftwerk?
questions.explanation: Radio-Aktivität (Radio-Activity) was a concept album by Kraftwerk built around the theme of radio broadcasting and, more ambiguously, radioactivity.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Arise" in 1991?
questions.explanation: Arise helped push Sepultura toward wider international recognition as one of the leading thrash and death metal bands of the era.

questions.prompt: In which year was "Don’t Play Me Cheap" by Ike & Tina Turner first released?
questions.explanation: Don't Play Me Cheap was one of several albums Ike & Tina Turner released early in their long recording partnership.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Everything Is Great" in 1979?
questions.explanation: Inner Circle built a strong reggae following through the late 1970s before achieving global mainstream success in the 1990s.

questions.prompt: Which of these albums is by Elvis Presley?
questions.explanation: Pot Luck was one of several albums Elvis Presley released after returning from military service in Germany.

questions.prompt: Which artist released the album "Boy Toy" in 1993?
questions.explanation: Boy Toy was a nickname tied to Madonna's mid-1980s image, famously referenced by a belt buckle she wore during the Like a Virgin era.
